flags.label: Gap in Care (>30 days)
flags.defense_argument: A 2495-day gap in treatment suggests the plaintiff's condition had resolved or was not serious enough to require ongoing care.
flags.plaintiff_counter: Treatment gaps are explained by scheduling constraints, insurance authorization delays, or documented symptom plateau — not resolution. Subsequent care resumption and continued objective findings confirm persistent injury.

flags.label: Conservative Care Only (No Surgery or Injection)

case_severity_index.profile: Profile: No objective imaging findings documented; ED-only treatment documented; Duration not fully documented.

anticipated_defense_arguments.argument: Defense may argue treatment interruption weakens continuity.
anticipated_defense_arguments.context_supported_in_records: Continuity concern appears in timeline chronology.

settlement_model_report.posture_text: This case presents significant settlement risk. Defense has multiple attack vectors. Key risk: 2495-day gap in treatment. Consider early mediation and realistic demand calibration based on documented injuries only. Case Severity Index: 1.4/10.

primary_risks: Conservative-only care, no imaging or specialist

adjustments.label: Conservative-only care, no imaging or specialist

negotiation_strategy.opening_strategy: Anchor near specials. Emphasize continuity of care and documented mechanism.
counter_positioning: Use consistency of presenting complaints as credibility anchor
Keep focus on documented objective evidence

demand_letter_draft.label: INTERNAL DRAFT — DO NOT EXPORT — EDIT BEFORE SENDING

blocks.A_LIABILITY_SUMMARY: Based on documented records, the claimant sustained injuries requiring medical treatment.
blocks.B_MEDICAL_OVERVIEW: Profile: No objective imaging findings documented; ED-only treatment documented; Duration not fully documented.
blocks.C_TREATMENT_COURSE: ED-only treatment documented; Duration not fully documented
blocks.E_DAMAGES: Total documented medical expenses: [See attached billing records]

attorney_notes: Preempt care gap by citing treating notes documenting continued symptoms, insurance delays, or financial constraints.
Anchor near documented medical expenses. Emphasize treatment consistency and mechanism.
